perturbation.original_text: 15. Law and Jurisdiction . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Korea (excluding conflict of laws provisions which may direct the application of another jurisdiction’s laws). All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) shall be finally resolved by arbitration to be held in Seoul, Korea and conducted in English under the Rules of Arbitration of the Intern ational Chamber of Commerce ; provided, however, that each party may enforce its or its affiliates’ intellectual property rights in any court of competent jurisdiction, including but not limit ed to equitable relief . The arbitral award shall be final and binding on the parties. Except to the extent entry of judgment and any subsequent enforcement may require disclosure, all matters relating to the arbitration, including the award, shall be held in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Export . Each party shall comply with all applicable US and other export laws, regulations and rules and, in particular, will not export or re -export Confidential Information without obtaining all required government licenses, approvals or waivers . 15. Law and Jurisdiction . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Korea (excluding conflict of laws provisions which may direct the application of another jurisdiction’s laws). All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) shall be finally resolved by arbitration to be held in Seoul, Korea and conducted in English under the Rules of Arbitration of the Intern ational Chamber of Commerce ; provided, however, that each party may enforce its or its affiliates’ intellectual property rights in any court of competent jurisdiction, including but not limit ed to equitable relief . The arbitral award shall be final and binding on the parties. Except to the extent entry of judgment and any subsequent enforcement may require disclosure, all matters relating to the arbitration, including the award, shall be held in confidence.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Law and Jurisdiction' clause, specifically the arbitration agreement, into the 'Export' section makes it less noticeable and could be argued as not being properly presented. This reduces the clarity of the agreement to arbitrate.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Federal Arbitration Act favors arbitration agreements, but it also requires that such agreements be presented clearly and conspicuously to ensure knowing consent. Placing the arbitration clause under 'Export' diminishes its prominence and might render it less enforceable under the FAA if challenged on the grounds of unconscionability or lack of clear notice. Furthermore, if a party argues they did not see the arbitration clause, a court may not be able to enforce the clause.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. L. 117–90 inserted “or as otherwise provided in chapter 4” before period at end. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date of 2022 Amendment Amendment by Pub. L. 117–90 applicable with respect to any dispute or claim that arises or accrues on or after Mar. 3, 2022 , see section 3 of Pub. L. 117–90 , set out as an Effective Date note under section 401 of this title . U.S. Code Toolbox Law about... perturbation.justification: The change moves the 'Law and Jurisdiction' clause into the 'Export' section. The Law Explanation states that placing the arbitration clause under 'Export' diminishes its prominence, potentially making it less enforceable under the FAA if challenged due to a lack of clear notice. This aligns with the Perturbation Explanation, which notes that the placement reduces the clarity of the agreement to arbitrate, making it less noticeable. Scraped Law Snippet 1, citing 9 U.S. Code § 2, emphasizes the validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of arbitration agreements. Moving the clause creates a structural flaw, as defined in the instructions, by placing a required legal disclosure (arbitration agreement) in an irrelevant section, making it difficult to locate or interpret, which contradicts the intent of 9 U.S. Code § 2.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall comply with all applicable US and other export laws, regulations and rules and, in particular, will not export or re -export Confidential Information without obtaining all required government licenses, approvals or waivers .
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS , the parties desire to evaluate the feasibility of the parties’ cooperation regarding KNOX Partnership (the “Purpose”). Each party shall comply with all applicable US and other export laws, regulations and rules and, in particular, will not export or re -export Confidential Information without obtaining all required government licenses, approvals or waivers .
perturbation.explanation: By moving the Export clause under the 'WHEREAS' section, it is implied that the export laws only apply if they 'desire to evaluate the feasibility'. This makes the requirement to abide by US export laws conditional and not universally binding.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Export Administration Regulations (EAR) mandates compliance with export control laws regardless of the parties' desires or the feasibility of a partnership. By placing the export compliance clause under the 'WHEREAS' section, the agreement implies that export compliance is conditional on the desire to evaluate a partnership, contradicting the EAR's unconditional requirement for compliance.

Nothing in these tools binds BIS or creates any rights, benefits, or defenses, substantive or procedural, that are enforceable by any party in any manner. If you find any errors, please email us.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw by placing the export compliance clause under the 'WHEREAS' section, making it appear conditional. The Law Explanation confirms that the Export Administration Regulations (EAR) mandate compliance regardless of the parties' desires. The changed text contradicts this unconditional requirement. Scraped Law Snippet 2, from the Bureau of Industry and Security, states that the Export Administration Regulations (EAR) are under 15 CFR 730-774, implying a mandatory and structured legal framework, not a conditional one based on a 'desire to evaluate'. This conditional phrasing weakens the legal obligation and could potentially lead to non-compliance, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Remedies . Each party acknowledges that any disclosure, use or misappropriation of Confidential Inform ation of another party in violation of this Agreement would cause such party irreparable harm for which there may be no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each party agrees that such other party shall have the right to apply to any court of competent jur isdiction for injunctive relief and specific performance, without prejudice to any remedies available to it at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Exclusions from Obligations . Confidential Information does not include, and t he obligations under Section 2 shall not apply to, information that such Recipient can evidence : (a) is, or later becomes, publicly available through no act or default of Recipient; (b) is rightfully in its possession prior to disclosure to Recipient by Discloser ; (c) is received in good faith by Recipient from a third party , free of any ob ligation of confidentiality ; (d) was communicated by such Discloser to an unaffiliated third party on an unrestricted basis ; or (e) is independently developed without use of Discloser’s Confidential Information . 11. Remedies . Each party acknowledges that any disclosure, use or misappropriation of Confidential Inform ation of another party in violation of this Agreement would cause such party irreparable harm for which there may be no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each party agrees that such other party shall have the right to apply to any court of competent jur isdiction for injunctive relief and specific performance, without prejudice to any remedies available to it at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Remedies' clause under 'Exclusions from Obligations' suggests that remedies are only applicable within the context of the exclusions, limiting their scope. This reduces the accessibility and understanding of the full legal remedies available to the parties.
perturbation.law_explanation: Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 359 outlines the availability of injunctive relief when monetary damages are inadequate. By burying the remedies clause under 'Exclusions from Obligations,' the agreement implies that injunctive relief is only available in cases involving excluded information, contradicting the general principle that injunctive relief is available for any breach causing irreparable harm. Therefore, the actual remedies are less clear because of this placement, going against the Restatement (Second) of Contracts.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving the remedies clause under 'Exclusions from Obligations' limits their scope, suggesting remedies are only applicable within the context of the exclusions. The law explanation supports this, indicating that this placement implies injunctive relief is only available in cases involving excluded information. This contradicts the general principle that injunctive relief is available for any breach causing irreparable harm. The 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition states that a legal contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. This definition aligns with the changed text, as the remedies clause is effectively buried, thus making the remedies less accessible and clear. The snippets do not help support this determination.
